CLINICIAN'S REPORT — INJURY DOCUMENTATION

Clinic: Mama Lucy Hospital, Outpatient
Clinician: Dr. R. Otieno (KMPDC Reg. ___) [REDACTED for synthetic demo case]
Date of examination: 25 June 2024
Time: 10:50 hrs
Patient (synthetic): Brian Kamau, 24 years, M

History (as reported by patient):
Patient states he was struck by a police officer with a baton at Parliament
Road on 24 June 2024 at approximately 1410 hrs during a public assembly.
Patient was subsequently held at Central Police Station overnight without
medical attention. Patient was permitted to attend this clinic on a brief
release for examination on 25 June 2024.

On examination:
- Right shoulder: 5 cm contusion, ecchymosis (yellow-green bruising
  consistent with 12-24 hour-old impact injury)
- Lower back, lumbar region: 8 cm linear contusion, ecchymosis, mild
  swelling — consistent with baton strike
- No fractures palpable; no neurological deficit
- Mild dehydration; patient reports no food or water for ~18 hours

Impression:
Soft-tissue contusions consistent with blunt-force trauma from a linear
implement. No evidence of life-threatening injury. Pain managed with
paracetamol 1 g. Patient advised follow-up in 72 hours.

This report is documentary evidence; the clinician is available for
deposition under the Oaths and Statutory Declarations Act if required.
